No Poverty.reason: The course's core competence in 'social responsibility and sustainable development' provides a weak, inferred link to supporting pro-poor development strategies (Target 1.b).

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to zero hunger.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to good health and well-being.

Quality Education.reason: As a required MBA course, it directly provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3), develops skills for employment and entrepreneurship (Target 4.4), and explicitly includes 'sustainable development' as a core competence (Target 4.7).

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly addresses gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to clean water and sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to affordable and clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course's focus on corporate finance, entrepreneurship, and financial services directly supports productive activities, job creation (Target 8.3), and access to financial services for all (Target 8.10).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Topics like 'Capital Budgeting' and 'Financial Innovation,' along with 'innovative ability' as a core competence, are fundamental to funding industry, infrastructure, and promoting innovation (Targets 9.3, 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course topic 'Market Efficiency' provides foundational knowledge relevant to improving the regulation and monitoring of global financial markets (Target 10.5), which can indirectly reduce inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to sustainable cities and communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The core competence of 'social responsibility, and sustainable development' directly supports encouraging companies to adopt sustainable practices and integrate sustainability information (Target 12.6).

Climate Action.reason: The mention of 'sustainable development' as a core competence provides a weak, inferred link to integrating climate change considerations into corporate financial planning (Target 13.2).

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to life below water.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to life on land.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The core competence of 'business ethics' directly addresses principles for reducing corporate corruption (Target 16.5) and building transparent, accountable institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's core competencies in developing a 'global view' and 'global leadership capabilities' are essential for enhancing global macroeconomic stability and policy coordination (Target 17.13).
